body:
## Summary
- Add an opt-in `agent-project-manager.yml` workflow for scheduled or manual project management scoring
- Score open issues and PRs for managed `priority/*` and `urgency/*` labels with dry-run default behavior
- Add optional label application and issue summary comments, plus docs and tests

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent test`

Closes #34

comments.body: @sepo-agent /orchestrate

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `review` (round 2/5).

manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777489912772 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxMjg2MzA0Nzc6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6Mzk6cmV2aWV3OjM5OjI -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR keeps workflow glue thin and puts behavior in typed `.agent/src` modules/CLI. It reuses `setup-agent-runtime`, `resolve-github-auth`, `scheduled-activity-gate`, and existing GitHub helpers in `.agent/src/github.ts`, extending them only with label removal helpers. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Adds `agent-project-manager.yml` to `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md` and documents all new `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_*` variables in `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes #34, whose discussion requested phase-one project management: opt-in by default, dry-run support, issue/PR scoring, optional labels, and a visible summary. The diff implements that scope and defers Projects integration. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are limited to the new project-management workflow, its CLI/scoring modules, minimal GitHub label helper additions, focused tests, and matching docs. No broad refactors or unrelated cleanup are present. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Uses deterministic scoring and direct `gh issue list` / `gh pr list` collection for phase one, with dry-run default and explicit opt-ins for label writes and summary comments. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | The docs add workflow/configuration descriptions without introducing stale per-file inventory tables. |

## Notes

- Strongest rubric drivers: the implementation matches the linked issue’s phase-one constraints, keeps the workflow thin, and updates the relevant docs alongside the behavior change.
- Verification: `npm --prefix .agent test` passed locally with 387 tests.
- Rubrics not scored because they do not genuinely apply to this PR: delegated route authorization, orchestrator one-shot follow-up defaults, default merge strategy, accepted workflow stop comments, stateful failure persistence, and rubric-authoring guidance.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** The PR satisfies the applicable selected rubrics. No rubric-specific blocking or warning findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #39 implements phase one of project management from issue #34.
- It adds an opt-in scheduled/manual workflow that scores open issues and PRs for managed `priority/*` and `urgency/*` labels.
- The implementation defaults to disabled and dry-run behavior, with optional label application and optional summary comments.
- It adds typed CLI/scoring code, focused tests, and documentation for the new workflow and configuration.

## Review
The reviews agree that the PR matches the requested phase-one scope and is generally well-structured. One actionable gap remains: the label mutation path is not covered by CLI regression tests.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Missing CLI coverage for label write path | WARNING | The dry-run path is tested, but the non-dry-run branch that creates/removes/adds labels is not exercised with fake `gh` calls. A broken mutation command could pass the current suite. |

## Progress
- Rubric review passed with no blocking or warning findings.
- PR discussion confirms the intended scope: opt-in, dry-run by default, deterministic scoring, optional labels, and summary output.
- Existing tests cover scoring, label planning, summary formatting, disabled mode, and dry-run CLI behavior.
- Posted one inline comment on `.agent/src/cli/agent-project-manager.ts` line 205 for the missing mutation-path test.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Missing CLI coverage for label write path</summary>

**Cause:** The risky branch starts when `applyLabels && !dryRun`; it creates managed labels, removes old managed labels, and applies new labels to issues and PRs. Current CLI tests verify dry-run avoids edits, but do not verify the actual mutation commands.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a CLI test using a fake `gh` binary with `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED=true`, `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DRY_RUN=false`, and `AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_APPLY_LABELS=true`. Assert expected `label list/create`, `issue edit --remove-label`, `issue edit --add-label`, `pr edit --remove-label`, and `pr edit --add-label` invocations with the correct repo and item numbers.

**Comments:** This is not a design blocker, but it covers the only branch that mutates repository state.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: Add the focused CLI regression test for the non-dry-run label application path; this is concrete branch-change work and safe for automation.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add CLI regression coverage for non-dry-run label application commands.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 3/5).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis recommends FIX_PR for a concrete unresolved minor issue: missing CLI regression coverage for the non-dry-run label mutation path. This is safe branch-change work within the round budget.

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-34/codex-25127393901`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Added focused CLI regression coverage for non-dry-run project label application.
- The fake `gh` test now verifies managed label list/create calls plus issue/PR stale-label removals and desired-label additions with repo and item numbers.
- Verification: `npm --prefix .agent test` passed with 394 tests.
@sepo-agent /answer did the orchestrator stop?

Yes. The orchestrator ran after the `fix-pr` pass, but it stopped instead of dispatching the next `review`.

Evidence:

- `fix-pr` completed at `2026-04-29 19:20 UTC`.
- A follow-up `Agent / Orchestrator` run started at `2026-04-29 19:20:16 UTC`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/actions/runs/25129044192
- Its log ended with: `Handoff stop: agent planner decision missing or invalid`.

So the automation chain stopped there. It did not continue to another review pass. The apparent cause was the planner run being cancelled/invalid after it tried to request permission for a `gh pr view` read, leaving no valid planner decision for the dispatch step.

> @sepo-agent /answer can you check my comments and come up with solutions?

@sepo-agent /answer the previous tag it did not work? try again checking my questions

The second tag worked. The first mention was inside a quoted block, and the router intentionally ignores quoted mentions; the live second `@sepo-agent /answer` is what triggered this run.

Solutions for your review comments:

- **Priority vs urgency:** simplify the user-facing labels. I’d keep one ranking axis for triage, e.g. `priority/p0` through `priority/p3`, and treat “urgency” as a reason in the summary, not a second label. That avoids overlapping `priority/*` and `urgency/*` labels while still letting the agent explain “needs review now”, “stale”, “blocked”, etc.
- **Can labels be assigned to PRs?** Yes. The PR already uses `gh pr edit <number> --add-label ...` / `--remove-label ...`, and the workflow has `pull-requests: write`. So PR label assignment is supported.
- **Cron documentation:** add an inline workflow comment and docs entry explaining the schedule. Current PR has `0 10 * * *`, which means daily at 10:00 UTC. If you want memory-like cadence, change it to every 6 hours, e.g. `17 */6 * * *`.
- **Ranking top priority tasks:** keep the scorer deterministic, but change the output to a clear “Top triage queue” sorted by score, with the top 5-10 issues/PRs and reasons.
- **Daily summary discussion integration:** don’t create a separate issue comment by default. Better: find today’s existing `Daily Summary — YYYY-MM-DD` discussion, then post the project-management update as a comment there. If today’s daily summary discussion does not exist yet, skip external posting and only write the Actions step summary. That avoids creating discussion entries out of order.
- **Implementation shape:** update `.agent/src/project-management.ts` to emit one triage label plus reasons, update `.github/workflows/agent-project-manager.yml` to run every 6 hours, add a helper to find today’s daily-summary discussion, and update docs/tests.

Recommended next step: run `/fix-pr` with this direction, because these are PR changes rather than just answers.
@sepo-agent /orchestrate

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `fix-pr` (round 2/5).

One comment: maybe another label that we should add is "effort/low medium high" to help people better triage the jobs

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-34/codex-25127393901`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

Implemented the PR follow-up changes on the existing branch.

- Replaced managed `urgency/*` labels with simpler `effort/low|medium|high` labels while keeping urgency-like signals as reasons/action score in the summary.
- Updated project-manager output to a “Top Triage Queue” sorted by priority and action score.
- Changed the workflow schedule to every 6 hours and documented the cron behavior.
- Changed optional posting from issue comments to commenting on today’s existing `Daily Summary — YYYY-MM-DD` discussion, skipping external posting if it does not exist.
- Added Discussion helper reuse and CLI tests for label mutation plus Daily Summary discussion commenting.
- Updated docs/configuration to match the new behavior.

Verification: `npm --prefix .agent test` passed with 395 tests.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 3/5).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded after addressing the human-requested PR changes and the prior review's test-coverage gap; PR reviewDecision is still CHANGES_REQUESTED, so a follow-up review is warranted within the round budget.

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #39 implements phase one of project-management automation from issue #34.
- It adds an opt-in scheduled/manual workflow that runs every 6 hours when enabled, asks an agent to assess open issues and PRs, and produces a top triage queue.
- The workflow can apply deterministic managed `priority/*` and `effort/*` label changes after validating the agent’s structured plan.
- It writes the summary to the Actions step summary and can optionally comment on today’s existing Daily Summary discussion.
- The PR also updates docs and adds focused tests for managed label parsing, label application, and summary posting.

## Review
The reviews agree the PR is well-scoped and matches the phase-one project-management direction, but one regression gap remains in the dry-run validation path. The rubric review passes; the Codex review found a concrete workflow correctness issue.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Dry-run mode silently accepts invalid label plans | WARNING | Missing or malformed structured `label_changes` output only fails when labels are actually applied. In default dry-run mode, a broken agent response can be reported as a clean zero-operation plan. |

## Progress
- Prior review feedback about non-dry-run label mutation coverage has been addressed.
- Human feedback was incorporated: `urgency/*` was replaced with `effort/*`, the schedule changed to every 6 hours, and optional summary posting targets the existing Daily Summary discussion.
- Documentation now covers workflow behavior, configuration, cron cadence, labels, dry-run mode, and discussion posting.
- Reviewer verification reports `npm --prefix .agent test` passing with 395 tests.
- Posted one inline comment on `.agent/src/cli/apply-project-management-labels.ts` line 41 for the dry-run invalid-plan behavior.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Dry-run mode silently accepts invalid label plans</summary>

**Cause:** `apply-project-management-labels.ts` only rejects an invalid structured plan under `!dryRun && applyLabels`. Because dry-run is the default safe preview mode, missing or malformed fenced JSON currently becomes `0 managed label operation(s) were planned`, which can make a broken prompt or agent response look successful.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate `plan.valid` before the dry-run/application branch. Prefer failing the step for missing or malformed structured output, while still allowing an explicit valid empty plan such as `{"label_changes":[]}`. If maintainers want dry-run to stay non-fatal, append an explicit invalid-plan warning to the summary/output and add a focused CLI regression test for that behavior.

**Comments:** Rubric review did not flag this, but the Codex review reproduced the behavior locally. This is minor but worth fixing because dry-run is the primary preview path.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The remaining issue is a concrete branch change with a narrow testable fix in the deterministic label-application CLI.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Validate missing or malformed `label_changes` plans in dry-run mode and add focused CLI coverage.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #39 implements phase one of project-management automation from issue #34.
- It adds an opt-in scheduled/manual workflow that assesses open issues and PRs, produces a top triage queue, and can apply managed `priority/*` and `effort/*` labels.
- The workflow stays off by default, supports dry-run preview, and separates prompt-driven assessment from deterministic label application.
- It writes summaries to the Actions step summary and can optionally comment on today’s existing Daily Summary discussion.
- The PR includes matching docs and tests for label parsing, label application, dry-run validation, mutation commands, and summary posting.

## Review
The current reviews agree that the previously flagged dry-run validation issue has been fixed. No blocking or warning findings remain, and both the rubric review and Codex review support shipping the current PR head.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | The dry-run invalid-plan gap is fixed, regression coverage was added, and the latest reviews report no unresolved blocking or warning findings. |

## Progress
- Prior mutation-path test coverage feedback was addressed.
- Human-requested follow-ups were incorporated: `effort/*` labels, 6-hour cadence, top triage queue, and Daily Summary discussion posting.
- The dry-run invalid-plan warning was addressed by rejecting invalid structured plans before dry-run or real label application.
- Tests now cover invalid dry-run output, invalid mutation output, explicit empty plans, and real fake-`gh` mutation commands.
- Latest reviewer verification reports `npm --prefix .agent ci` and `npm --prefix .agent test` passing with 396 tests.

## Recommended Next Step

@sepo-agent /answer 

what to do if i want to 
1. enable the project management 
2. activate it immediately

After PR #39 is merged, do this:

1. **Enable scheduled project management**
   - Set repo variable:
     ```bash
     gh variable set AGENT_PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED --repo self-evolving/repo --body true
     ```
   - This enables the scheduled workflow every 6 hours: `17 */6 * * *`.

2. **Activate it immediately**
   - Run the workflow manually:
     ```bash
     gh workflow run agent-project-manager.yml \
       --repo self-evolving/repo \
       -f enabled=true \
       -f dry_run=true \
       -f apply_labels=false \
       -f post_summary=false \
       -f discussion_category=General \
       -f limit=100
     ```

Recommended first run: keep `dry_run=true` and `apply_labels=false` so you can inspect the summary without changing labels.

When you want it to actually write labels:

```bash
gh workflow run agent-project-manager.yml \
  --repo self-evolving/repo \
  -f enabled=true \
  -f dry_run=false \
  -f apply_labels=true \
  -f post_summary=true \
  -f discussion_category=General \
  -f limit=100
```

`post_summary=true` only comments on today’s existing `Daily Summary — YYYY-MM-DD` discussion. If that discussion does not exist yet, it will just write the Actions step summary.

## Rubrics Update

Updated `agent/rubrics` from PR #39.

Added one durable rubric:

- `validate-preview-contracts`: dry-run/preview/read-only automation paths should validate the same structured contracts as mutation paths and avoid misleading clean no-op results for malformed input.

Source: trusted maintainer/admin feedback in PR #39, specifically https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/39#issuecomment-4348043093.

Validation passed: `node .agent/dist/cli/rubrics/validate.js --dir .../agent-rubrics` validated 13 rubrics.

The run failed in the **Run project manager agent** step, not in the gate/auth/config steps.

Root cause: the project-manager agent tried to run:

```bash
gh issue list --repo self-evolving/repo --state open ...
gh pr list --repo self-evolving/repo --state open ...
```

but the agent was running with `permission_mode: approve-reads`. Those `gh issue list` / `gh pr list` commands triggered an approval prompt, and the approval result was `abort`, so the ACPX session was cancelled:

```text
[tool] Run gh pr list ... (failed)
[tool] Run gh issue list ... (failed)
[done] cancelled
acpx completed exit_code=5
Process completed with exit code 1
```

So this was **not** because project management was disabled, and not because labels/posting were enabled. The workflow config resolved as:

```text
dry_run: true
apply_labels: false
post_summary: false
limit: 100
```

The failure is that the agent could not read the open issues/PRs under the current execution permission policy.

Best fix: don’t make the agent call `gh issue list` / `gh pr list` directly. Add a deterministic workflow step before the agent that fetches open issues/PRs using the already-resolved `GH_TOKEN`, writes them to files, and passes those files into the project-manager prompt. Then the agent only reads local files, and `approve-reads` is enough.

Quick workaround: change the project-manager `run-agent-task` step from `permission_mode: approve-reads` to `permission_mode: approve-all`, but I’d prefer the prefetch-file approach because it keeps the agent’s permissions narrower and more predictable.

>Quick workaround: change the project-manager run-agent-task step from permission_mode: approve-reads to permission_mode: approve-all, but I’d prefer the prefetch-file approach because it keeps the agent’s permissions narrower and more predictable.

@sepo-agent /orchestrate can you do a quick patch for that fix?

reviews.body: @sepo-agent /answer can you check my comments and come up with solutions?

title: Add opt-in project management scoring workflow
